text: Figure 10.14 $1 \times 1$ convolution. To change the number of channels without spatial pooling, we apply a $1 \times 1$ kernel. Each output channel is computed by taking a weighted sum of all of the channels at the same position, adding a bias, and passing through an activation function. Multiple output channels are created by repeating this operation with different weights and biases.
